description: Optimal work-to-solution (Monte Carlo sweep units) for population annealing vs PT-ICM (parallel tempering with isoenergetic cluster moves = Houdayer-PT), on 3D Edwards-Anderson Gaussian instances. PT-ICM is the classical baseline against which D-Wave's strongest optimization scaling-advantage claim (Munoz Bauza & Lidar, PRL 134, 160601, 2025) is measured.
metric: optimal work-to-solution (sweep units; budget scanned, minimum taken), median over 4 instances per size
ground_truth: heavy-PA floor (validated == brute force at N<=27)

rows.note: PT-ICM did not reach the best-known energy at any tested budget (<= 512000 sweep units); PA solved it.

caveat: 3D cubic EA Gaussian, our own kernel; NOT the 2D degree-5 high-precision graph of the D-Wave benchmark. The pattern (PA >> PT-ICM) is the point; absolute transfer to the D-Wave instance class is an untested hypothesis.
